demographics.living_situation: lives in the family home; parent present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Parent calling] Please help — I just found my son slumped on the floor, he's barely breathing and his lips are turning blue! I can't wake him up at all. There are empty pill packets all around him.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Found him unresponsive about 15 minutes ago, breathing very slowly, and he won't wake up no matter what I do.

symptom_narrative.onset: found like this about 15 minutes ago; the parent doesn't know exactly when it started
symptom_narrative.duration: at least 15 minutes and he hasn't woken up
symptom_narrative.character: completely limp and unresponsive, not reacting to shouting or shaking, breathing very slowly and shallowly
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the parent says it's terrifying — he looks like he's dying, his lips and fingertips are going blue
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: found slumped on the bedroom floor
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: no change — he has stayed unconscious the whole time
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing the parent has tried has woken him
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the parent thinks his breathing is getting slower
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: not moving, not speaking, skin feels cool and clammy
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the parent says he has struggled with drug problems before and has been using again recently

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing problems with drugs / painkiller and sedative use
past_medical_history.how_managed: on-and-off contact with support services, per the parent

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: the parent found empty packets of a strong painkiller (oxycodone) and a sedative (diazepam) near him

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: adult son living in the family home; parent found him and made the call; history of substance use volunteered by the parent

objective_data_offered.value: he does not wake up or respond at all when I shout his name or shake him
he's only taking a few slow, shallow breaths — long gaps between them
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer's eyeball estimate of breathing rate, not a measured value

objective_data_offered.value: lips and fingertips look blue/dusky; his face is pale and his skin looks sweaty; the black centres of his eyes look like tiny pinpoints
empty blister packs of oxycodone and diazepam and some drug paraphernalia on the bedside table and floor beside him
